channel.title: Nischay Mohan — a digital garden
channel.description: A learn-in-public garden on how payment systems work, the geopolitics of money, and AI as leverage over financial systems.

item.title: The Interchange Highway: what really happens in a card transaction
item.description: The four-party model, the fees skimmed at each hop, and where the Merchant Discount Rate actually goes. With a step-by-step diagram.

item.title: Why Amex doesn't fly in the UK
item.description: A three-party network in a four-party world — what American Express's model reveals about the real trade-off behind card acceptance: rewards vs. reach.

item.title: Ten years ago I wrote about BRICS 2.0 — what I got right and what I missed
item.description: Revisiting decade-old predictions about alternatives to the dollar-based payment rails: what I got right, what I missed, and why.

item.title: How money moves
item.description: Authorization vs. clearing vs. settlement, in plain language — the three steps most people compress into the word "payment".

item.title: The float is where the money sleeps
item.description: The gap between "authorized" and "settled" isn't a bug — it's a business, and a risk, all its own.

item.title: SWIFT moves messages, not money
item.description: A tiny distinction that explains a surprising amount about sanctions and who holds power over the rails.
